title: The Abolitionist Issue 35: Defunding Policing
description: Critical Resistance's newspaper on PIC abolition organizing post-2020 uprisings, focused on defunding strategies and movement-building.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 36: Pathways Toward Freedom
description: Critical Resistance newspaper exploring paths to abolition through freedom struggles, prisoner support, and survivor-centered approaches.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 37: Housing Justice & PIC Abolition
description: The connections between housing justice organizing and prison-industrial-complex abolition.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 39: Reproductive Justice
description: Reproductive justice as a framework for abolition; coverage of incarcerated pregnancy, post-Dobbs criminalization, and reproductive autonomy.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 40: Control Units
description: Long-term solitary confinement and control units, including coverage of political prisoners held in indefinite isolation.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 41: Ecological Justice
description: Ecological dimensions of incarceration: environmental racism, prison-siting in toxic zones, and climate movement — abolition links.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 42: Anti-War Organizing
description: Anti-war organizing through an abolitionist lens; coverage of Palestine solidarity prosecutions and the criminalization of protest.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: The Abolitionist Issue 43: Seeds Through Concrete — Censorship & Repression
description: Censorship and repression of inside organizing; prison mail bans, book bans, and the criminalization of incarcerated writers.
collection: Critical Resistance — The Abolitionist

title: Search and Destroy: Fred Hampton/Mark Clark Commission Report
description: 1970 commission report on the police assassination of Black Panther Party Chairman Fred Hampton and Mark Clark in Chicago, December 4, 1969.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Hampton v. Hanrahan — 7th Circuit Brief
description: Federal appellate brief in the civil-rights case brought by survivors and the families of Fred Hampton and Mark Clark against Chicago authorities.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Hampton COINTELPRO Booklet
description: Documentary booklet on the FBI COINTELPRO program's role in the killing of Fred Hampton and Mark Clark.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Chicago Police Torture: A Legal and Political History
description: Comprehensive history of the Jon Burge torture regime at Chicago's Area 2/3, and the decades-long fight by survivors and counsel for accountability and reparations.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Goldston OPS Report on Burge Torture (1990)
description: Chicago Office of Professional Standards report by Michael Goldston concluding that Area 2 Commander Jon Burge and detectives under his command engaged in systematic torture of Black suspects.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Puerto Rican Political Prisoners (PLO Factsheet)
description: People's Law Office factsheet on Puerto Rican political prisoners and FALN defendants, including legal status, charges, and post-1999 clemency context.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: 13 Years of Police Torture (Chicago Tribune, 1992)
description: Chicago Tribune investigation documenting thirteen years of police torture under Jon Burge in Chicago's Area 2/3.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Police Torture Shadow Report to UN CAT (2007)
description: Shadow report submitted by People's Law Office and allies to the UN Committee Against Torture on Chicago police torture and the U.S. obligation to investigate and provide reparations.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: Hampton Trial Document (December 3, 1976)
description: Afternoon transcript fragment from the Hampton v. Hanrahan civil trial, December 3, 1976.
collection: People's Law Office — Hampton, Torture, FALN

title: First IWW Convention Proceedings (1905)
description: Proceedings of the founding convention of the Industrial Workers of the World, Chicago, June–July 1905. Includes speeches by Big Bill Haywood, Eugene Debs, Mother Jones, and Daniel De Leon.

collection: Marxists Internet Archive — IWW & Smith Act

title: IWW 1907 Convention Proceedings
description: Proceedings of the Industrial Workers of the World's third national convention, 1907.
publisher: Industrial Workers of the World (MIA mirror)
collection: Marxists Internet Archive — IWW & Smith Act

title: The Sweat Box (Foster, 1909)
description: William Z. Foster's pamphlet on his jail experience during the 1909 IWW Spokane free-speech fight.

title: The Rockpile (Foster, 1910)
description: William Z. Foster's account of forced-labor punishment at a rockpile during his Spokane jail term for IWW free-speech organizing.

title: 47 Days in Jail (Foster, 1910)
description: William Z. Foster's account of 47 days of confinement during the 1909–1910 IWW Spokane free-speech fight.
collection: Marxists Internet Archive — IWW & Smith Act

title: Larkin Trial Coverage (1920)
description: Coverage of the New York criminal-anarchy prosecution of Irish labor leader James Larkin, sentenced to Sing Sing in 1920.

title: Speech on Revolution (Gitlow, 1919)
description: Speech by Benjamin Gitlow that became the basis of the criminal-anarchy prosecution decided in Gitlow v. New York (1925).

title: Debs Indicted (Espionage Act, 1918)
description: Contemporary coverage of Eugene V. Debs's 1918 indictment under the Espionage Act for the Canton, Ohio anti-war speech.

title: The IWW Bogey (Debs, 1918)
description: Eugene V. Debs's defense of the IWW against wartime persecution, written in 1918.
collection: Marxists Internet Archive — IWW & Smith Act

title: Centralia: A Story of Wartime Murder (1922)
description: IWW account of the 1919 Centralia Massacre and the wartime persecution of Wesley Everest and Wobbly defendants.

title: The Haywood Affair (Loebl, 1921)
description: Loebl's 1921 essay on Big Bill Haywood's flight to Soviet Russia while on bail in the federal IWW prosecutions.

title: Cannon on Haywood (Obituary, 1928)
description: James P. Cannon's obituary essay for Big Bill Haywood, May 22, 1928.
collection: Marxists Internet Archive — IWW & Smith Act
